text: are new to me. Haven’t hated anyone yet .” “It’s an ugly feeling.” Though her appetite had fled, she took another bite. She needed her strength for this journey. “He allows everyone only a handful of crops, and forces all those people to share one water pump. Couples aren’t allowed more than one child . “Sometimes I wonder why he didn’t just kill us all…but he enjoys it, doesn’t he? The torture. Our terror .” “I don’t know if he enjoys it. He’s…nearly blank. Doesn’t show much .” “But he has to feel something . Why else would he do all of it ?” “Maybe it’s because he feels nothing, and he knows it .” “He feels,” she insisted, recalling his disgust after he raped her. “You do. Why couldn’t he ?” “He’s behind us, now.” Ronin slid beside her and put his arm around her shoulders, drawing her against him. “Whatever he’s done, we must leave it in the past and look to our future .” Lara nodded and leaned her head on his shoulder. What about Gary and Kate, with their second child on the way? They couldn’t just leave it in the past. There was nothing Lara could do for them, nothing she could do for anyone back there. All she could do was look ahead, to the life she’d have with Ronin. To her own happiness . She kissed him. “Thank you for giving me a future to look forward to .” “It’s nothing you haven’t already given me, Lara Brooks .” After eating some more meat and draining the canteen, she forced herself to her feet. Her body threatened to collapse at the exertion, but she didn’t give in. She couldn’t. They had to keep moving. She refilled the canteen from one of the large containers and hooked it on her belt, shrugging out of the heavy coat . Ronin gathered their belongings as Lara wrapped her scarf around her head. Together, they went out into the sweltering heat. Sweat rolled over her skin, trickling between her breasts and down her back. How could anyone deal with such wild variations in temperature every day, without consistent shelter ? As they walked, they passed the twisted, skeletal remains of trees. After having seen the greenery in the Bot District, she could almost imagine what this area might have looked like, the beauty this land must once have possessed . The rugged terrain soon put an end to such musings. The ground dipped and rose with increasing severity, and large, scattered rocks grew more frequent. Several times, her foot caught on the uneven ground, causing her to stumble. She stopped occasionally to catch her breath and take a drink, often at Ronin’s insistence. It took all her focus to keep her feet moving; she had no brainpower left to track her thirst or breathing, on top of that . As they progressed, the dirt gave way to sparse patches of green grass. It was strange to see outside of Cheyenne. Part of her didn’t believe it could exist anywhere else . She stopped at the crest of a tall hill, eyes wide. A stream wound through the small valley below, and along its banks grew clusters of lush grass, trees, and bushes . “It used to look like this all over didn’t it?” she asked, tugging the scarf away from her mouth . “I think it did, in a lot of places .” “Wish I could’ve seen it. Is the rest of the world anything like this spot ?” “There’s a lot more vegetation to the north,” he replied, “but the Dust goes on for a long, long way. Near a thousand miles across in any direction .” A thousand miles? Why did she think she could do this? She was ready to fall over, dead, after a single night . He watched her, as though puzzling out her thoughts. “We’re in the northwestern corner of it now. We’ve a long way to walk, still, but not nearly a thousand miles .” “I can’t walk night and day, Ronin .” “You don’t have to .” “Then we need to get on some kind of schedule for rest. I won’t be able to keep doing this. As well as I’ve been eating, I’m still not prepared for traveling like this .” “Glad I didn’t take you on scrap runs ?” “No. We could’ve taken our time on those. No reason to rush. But this…we’re running from something, even if we think it’s already long behind us .” “Best to treat it that way, for now. Knowing what we left behind …” “I just know I can’t keep up this pace, Ronin. We have to either rest at night and walk during the day, or the other way around. You can’t expect me to do both .” “I don’t expect you to, Lara. You’re worn out, and I’ve taken you away from the only place you’ve ever known. There is uncertainty both before us and behind us. I’m not going to walk you to death when the only reason we left is because I care for you .” She turned away, eyes watering. It wasn’t I love you , but it was pretty damned close . Ronin was in front of her a second later, lifting a hand to gently wipe the tears from her cheeks . “Got sand in my eyes,” she rasped . “Of course you did,” he replied, smiling. “I don’t truly understand what you are experiencing. I can’t. But I see, and hear, and smell, and touch, and I know how the things you feel affect you, even if I don’t fully comprehend what those things are .” Lara offered him a return smile before she pulled her scarf back up. “We’d better get going. Lots of ground to cover, still .” He nodded, surveying the land. “We’ll start veering north, away from the main road. It’ll be rough going, but it’s safer .” Barely
